verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein (p24δ subfamily) whose dominant steady-state compartment is the ER–ERGIC–Golgi axis, where it acts as a cargo receptor for COPII/COPI vesicle trafficking. However, multiple authoritative sources (RefSeq, GeneCards, Wikipedia) explicitly annotate plasma membrane localization alongside Golgi cisternae, and mechanistic studies show the lumenal GOLD domain partially overrides ER retention to drive a PM pool. Presynaptic membrane localization is also documented in neurons. This constitutes a genuine dual-localization (ER/Golgi dominant + documented minority PM pool), where the protein reaches the PM by its own TM anchor. The extracellular/lumenal face of the single-pass TM domain is accessible from outside the cell when the protein is at the PM. Not secreted-only, not purely endomembrane-resident. No evidence of cell-death or oncogenic-state-specific ecto-pool; no germline/tissue restriction; not a lysosomal TM protein; no stable TM-partner post-translational tethering required.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The fraction of TMED10 at the PM vs. ER/Golgi under baseline conditions varies by cell type and is not precisely quantified; direct surface-biotinylation or flow-cytometry data on intact non-permeabilized cells for human TMED10 were not retrieved.
